title: RCC - Most Repeated Questions (20+ asked every exam)
content:
✅ MOST REPEATED RCC QUESTIONS - TSPSC AEE

Q1. Partial safety factor for concrete in limit state of collapse?
Ans: 1.5

Q2. Partial safety factor for steel?
Ans: 1.15

Q3. Maximum strain in concrete at failure (limit state)?
Ans: 0.0035

Q4. xu,max/d for Fe415?
Ans: 0.48

Q5. xu,max/d for Fe500?
Ans: 0.46

Q6. xu,max/d for Fe250?
Ans: 0.53

Q7. Mu,lim for Fe415 beam?
Ans: 0.138 fck.b.d²

Q8. Mu,lim for Fe500 beam?
Ans: 0.133 fck.b.d²

Q9. Minimum tension reinforcement in beam?
Ans: As,min = 0.85bd/fy

Q10. Maximum reinforcement in beam?
Ans: 4% of bD (gross area)

Q11. Max spacing of shear reinforcement?
Ans: 0.75d or 300mm (whichever less)

Q12. One-way slab condition (Ly/Lx)?
Ans: > 2

Q13. Distribution steel for HYSD bars in slab?
Ans: 0.12% of bD

Q14. Span/depth ratio - Simply supported slab?
Ans: 20

Q15. Span/depth ratio - Cantilever?
Ans: 7

Q16. Span/depth ratio - Continuous?
Ans: 26

Q17. Short column condition?
Ans: Le/D ≤ 12 or Le/b ≤ 12

Q18. Axial load capacity of short column?
Ans: Pu = 0.4fck.Ac + 0.67fy.Asc

Q19. Min reinforcement in column?
Ans: 0.8% of gross area

Q20. Max reinforcement in column?
Ans: 6% (4% practical)

Q21. Lateral ties spacing?
Ans: Least of: (i) least lateral dimension (ii) 16×dia of bar (iii) 300mm

Q22. Development length Ld for Fe415 in M20?
Ans: 47φ (approx)

Q23. Punching shear checked at?
Ans: d/2 from face of column

Q24. One-way shear checked at?
Ans: d from face of column

Q25. BM in footing calculated at?
Ans: Face of column

Q26. Clear cover for beam?
Ans: 25mm

Q27. Clear cover for column?
Ans: 40mm

Q28. Clear cover for footing?
Ans: 50mm

Q29. Modular ratio for M20?
Ans: m = 280/(3×7) = 13.33

Q30. Characteristic strength means?
Ans: 95% of samples exceed this value (5% failure probability)

--- FORMULAS TO REMEMBER ---
• Design strength of concrete = 0.447fck
• Design strength of steel = 0.87fy
• Load combination: 1.5DL + 1.5LL
• Ld = 0.87fy×φ/(4×τbd)
• τbd for M20 deformed bars = 1.2×1.6 = 1.92 N/mm²
